extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3, which requires the full 12-condition conjunction (ckd_dialysis, seizure_history, immunosuppressed, diabetes, g6pd_deficiency, pregnancy, polypharmacy, elderly, anticoagulated, hypertension, peptic_ulcer, heart_failure all true). For output rec_drug_b, both r2 (priority 50, matches because ckd_dialysis is true) and r4 (priority 90) match, and the highest-priority matching rule wins, so r4 dominates and rec_drug_b=false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=false. In every other input, rec_drug_a=false (no other rule sets it, default is false), making the conjunction trivially false. Therefore rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b can never be jointly true across the entire input space. The property holds.
